findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (100% marked accessible). Fare data is published.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 2 kinds of issues: 0 error, 1 warning, and 1 informational instances in total.

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 195 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.
